name: Usui Sadamitsu
description: Usui Sadamitsu, also known as Sadamitsu no Yasuna, was a legendary warrior and retainer of Minamoto no Yorimitsu (Raiko) during the Heian period. He is one of the Shitenno (Four Heavenly Kings) serving Raiko, famed for their exceptional martial prowess and loyalty. Sadamitsu is depicted in various tales as a brave, resourceful, and sometimes eccentric figure, often undertaking dangerous missions involving demons and supernatural creatures. He is celebrated for his strength, skill with the sword, and unwavering dedication to his lord. His origins are somewhat shrouded in legend, but he is generally accepted as a historical figure whose exploits were embellished over time, solidifying his place in Japanese folklore. Sadamitsu's stories frequently involve him confronting and defeating powerful oni (demons) and other monstrous entities, safeguarding the realm from their malevolent influence. He embodies the ideals of a loyal samurai, combining physical strength with cunning and a strong moral compass. Sadamitsu's legacy continues to inspire stories and artwork, solidifying his position as a prominent figure in Japanese mythology, symbolizing courage, loyalty, and the eternal struggle against evil. His adventures have been recounted in various forms of art, literature, and theatre, ensuring his enduring presence in Japanese cultural heritage. He often acts as the level-headed member of the Raiko's group, balancing the more impulsive members.
subtitle: Loyal warrior and demon slayer
achievements: Slaying the Shuten-doji with Raiko and others
Demonstrating unwavering loyalty to his lord

keyMyths.title: Defeating Shuten-doji
keyMyths.description: Sadamitsu, along with Raiko and the other Shitenno, infiltrated Shuten-doji's mountain fortress and defeated the powerful oni after tricking him with poisoned sake. This victory is one of the most famous tales associated with the Raiko group.

keyMyths.title: The Tsuchigumo Encounter
keyMyths.description: Sadamitsu is often credited with playing a key role in the defeat of the giant spider demon, Tsuchigumo. He might have discovered its lair or been instrumental in the final confrontation, showcasing his bravery and skill.
keyMyths.source: Various Noh plays and folk tales

keyMyths.title: Loyalty to Yorimitsu
keyMyths.description: Many stories highlight Sadamitsu's unwavering loyalty to Minamoto no Yorimitsu, emphasizing his willingness to risk his life to protect his lord from both physical and supernatural dangers. This loyalty is a defining characteristic of his heroic persona.

sources.author: Helen Craig McCullough
sources.work: The Taiheiki: A Chronicle of Medieval Japan
sources.description: Provides historical context for the era in which Sadamitsu's legends developed.
sources.text: Provides historical context for the era in which Sadamitsu's legends developed.
